introduction:
A Registered Will is a pivotal legal instrument that ensures your assets and properties are distributed precisely according to your final wishes. By formally registering the Will under the Indian Registration Act, 1908, you provide it with an undisputed legal backing, significantly minimizing the chances of future familial disputes or fraudulent claims in Maharashtra.

Registration ensures the document is safely preserved in the official government custody (Book No. 3), providing an authentic reference that cannot be easily tampered with, destroyed, or forged.
quickFacts:
Important Pre-Requisites
  • Optional but Recommended: While not legally mandatory, a registered Will is infinitely harder to contest in a court of law.
  • No Stamp Duty: In Maharashtra, there is no stamp duty on a Will, only nominal registration and scanning fees.
  • Two Witnesses: The physical presence of two independent and reliable witnesses at the Sub-Registrar Office is strictly mandatory.
  • Medical Fitness: A certificate from a doctor verifying the testator's "Sound Mind" is highly advised to preempt any claims of mental incapacity.
documentsRequired:
Comprehensive Document Checklist

Note: Always carry the original documents along with at least two sets of self-attested photocopies to the Sub-Registrar Office.
For the Testator (Will Maker)
  • Primary ID: Original Aadhaar Card (mandatory for biometric authentication).
  • Secondary ID: PAN Card or Voter ID.
  • The Will: The original drafted Will on plain paper.
  • Medical Proof: A recent Medical Fitness Certificate from a registered doctor.
  • Photographs: 2 recent passport-size photographs.
For the Two Witnesses
  • Identity Proof: Original Aadhaar Card or Voter ID of both witnesses.
  • Photographs: 2 passport-size photographs of each witness.
  • Physical Presence: Both witnesses must be present simultaneously with the testator.
onlineApplication:
Step 1: Online Pre-Registration & Fee Payment

  1. Draft the Will: Consult a legal professional to draft your Will clearly, mentioning all assets, properties, the appointed executor, and the beneficiaries.
  2. Visit the Official Portal: Access the IGR Maharashtra portal.
  3. Data Entry: Navigate to the 'Public Data Entry (PDE)' section. Enter the demographic details of the testator, witnesses, and the abstract details of the Will.
  4. Fee Payment: Generate the e-Challan and pay the mandatory registration and scanning fees online using the GRAS portal.
  5. Book an Appointment: Once the payment is successful, use the 'e-Stepin' facility to select your preferred Sub-Registrar Office (SRO) and book a time slot for physical verification. Print the appointment slip.
offlineApplication:
Step 2: Physical Registration at the SRO

  1. Timely Arrival: Visit the designated Sub-Registrar Office exactly 15 minutes before your scheduled appointment time.
  2. Document Submission: Present the printed appointment slip, fee receipt, the original draft Will, and all original ID proofs to the receiving officer.
  3. Sub-Registrar Verification: The Sub-Registrar will formally interact with the testator to confirm their identity, ensure they are acting of their own free will without coercion, and verify their mental soundness.
  4. Biometric Authentication: The digital photographs and biometric fingerprints of the testator and both witnesses will be captured using the official system.
  5. Final Endorsement: The Sub-Registrar will sign and stamp the document. The registered Will is then scanned into the government database (Book No. 3) and the original is returned to the testator.
afterApplying:
Post-Registration Actions

  • Safe Custody: Store the original registered Will in a highly secure location, such as a bank safe deposit locker.
  • Executor Notification: Inform your appointed executor or a highly trusted family member about the exact location of the Will.
  • Confidentiality: Note that the contents of a registered Will remain strictly confidential. The Sub-Registrar office will not issue copies to anyone or make it available online while the testator is alive.
  • Future Amendments: If you wish to change your beneficiaries or assets later, you can execute a new registered Will (which automatically supersedes the old one) or register a Codicil.
